mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market momentum that triggers immediate risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE increases by over 0.4pp within a calendar month.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing construction activity combined with rising retail sales volume indicates robust consumer demand, which expands corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power if the joint rank correlation between HOUST and RSAFS shows no significant relationship with 20-day SPX returns (p > 0.10) across a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production contraction, supply chain bottlenecks amplify input cost pressures, forcing margin compression that suppresses S&P 500 equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The mechanism is falsified if S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in over 60% of quarters following two consecutive months of declining industrial production.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth directly increases aggregate corporate revenue expectations, leading to immediate upward repricing of equity indices and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if the Spearman rank correlation between quarterly GDP growth revisions and 5-day SPX forward returns remains below 0.05 over a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5 percent, the cost of capital becomes prohibitive for leveraged corporate expansion, triggering a sustained valuation multiple contraction that depresses S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if S&P 500 60-day forward returns average positive values in more than 50% of months where the federal funds rate remains above 4.5 percent for at least 30 days.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, which boosts consumer spending power and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month period.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails if the interaction term between monthly PAYEMS changes and UNRATE changes explains less than 1% of the variance in 20-day SPX returns across a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sustained inflationary pressure above 3.0 percent annualized, real wage growth stagnates and consumer purchasing power erodes, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months where the CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth rate exceeds 3.0 percent.
